message:
fix(extensions): fix exec wrapper double-buffering that lost stdout/stderr on close

The __wrapExecLike finish() callback was joining stdoutChunks/stderrChunks
internally but the close handler was also referencing undefined stdout/stderr
variables instead of re-joining the chunks, causing empty output to be
passed to the callback when the child process closed.

- Pass outStr/errOutStr as optional parameters to finish() so callers
  can provide pre-joined output
- In the close handler, join stdoutChunks/stderrChunks before passing
  to finish() instead of referencing undefined variables
- finish() falls back to joining chunks itself when parameters are
  not provided (preserving backward compatibility for error paths)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
